About Us Start Your Dental Assistant Career Today Frisco Advanced Dental Assisting School was created to offer an accelerated, comprehensive program that equips students with the knowledge, technical skills, and confidence to thrive as dental assistants. In just 10 weeks , our hybrid curriculum combines: Online learning (MWF, 1. 5 hours each day) — Flexible, self-paced modules you can complete on your schedule.

Over 10 weeks, you’ll learn the skills needed to assist chairside with a dentist, sterilize equipment and instruments, perform lab work, expose and mount X-rays, assist hygienists, schedule appointments, and help manage many aspects of a dental office. Program Structure Our program combines 4. 5 hours of flexible, self-paced online learning each week (Monday, Wednesday, and Friday — 1. 5 hours each day) with 2 hours of in-person, hands-on training every Sunday.

You’ll learn on the latest technology and gain confidence working with the tools, techniques, and workflows used in today’s orthodontic and surgical dental offices. Your Path to Certification Our 10-week program meets all Texas state requirements and prepares you to take the steps needed to become a Registered Dental Assistant (RDA). Upon completion, you’ll receive a Certificate in Dental Assisting and be ready to launch your new career with skills that set you apart.

Chapter 3: Radiology & CT Imaging Essentials This chapter covers radiology and CT imaging essentials. You’ll discover proper digital X-ray techniques, CBCT scanning, anatomy identification, and safe, accurate positioning for high-quality imaging. Chapter 4: iTero & Intraoral Scanning This chapter is dedicated to iTero and intraoral scanning. You’ll explore its applications in dentistry, practice hands-on scanning, and learn how to upload, share, and review digital files for case planning.

Chapter 5: Orthodontic Assisting & Digital Invisalign Workflow This chapter dives into orthodontic assisting and Invisalign workflows. You’ll assist with attachment bonding, case setups, attachments, and digital progress documentation. Chapter 6: Implantology – Surgical Assisting Basics This chapter introduces implantology and surgical assisting basics. You’ll learn sterile field setup, instrument preparation, implant anatomy, and patient monitoring for surgical procedures.

--- Full Arch or Full Mouth Dental Implants ---
Transform Your Smile with Full Arch or Full Mouth Dental Implants Tired of loose dentures or missing teeth affecting your confidence and health. Full arch or full mouth dental implants offer a stable, permanent solution to restore your smile and improve your quality of life.

This provides a secure, comfortable fit, allowing you to eat, speak, and smile with confidence. Full mouth implants replace all the teeth in the upper or lower arch, providing a seamless and beautiful result. Why Choose Dental Implants. Dental implants do more than restore your smile. They help preserve the natural shape of your face by preventing bone loss, which can occur when teeth are missing.

This advanced approach allows us to place implants with exceptional accuracy—often within 1 degree of the planned position.

--- Implant Supported Bridge ---
Implant Support Bridge Replacing several missing teeth doesnt have to mean dealing with uncomfortable removable dentures. An implant supported bridge offers a stable, long-term solution without compromising your natural teeth.

Over time, dentures can cause facial sagging, but implants help maintain a youthful appearance by stimulating your jawbone, much like natural tooth roots do. This prevents the bone from shrinking, ensuring that your facial structure remains intact. At Frisco Dental Implant Center , we specialize in creating fixed implant-supported arches that feel just like your natural teeth. Our team of experts works closely with you to ensure that you achieve the best possible results.

Unlike traditional bridges, which require filing down healthy teeth for support, implant supported bridges anchor directly to dental implants placed in your jaw. They feel more secure, function like natural teeth, and are not susceptible to tooth decay. This makes them an excellent choice for patients seeking a fixed, durable option to restore their smile and oral health. Click below to find out if you’re a candidate for an implant supported bridge. At Frisco Dental Implant Center , we offer Same Day Teeth , a solution that allows you to leave surgery with a beautiful, functional smile — on the very same day. Traditional dental implant treatments often require months of healing before final teeth are placed. With Same Day Teeth, also known as immediate-load dental implants, we streamline the process by placing a fixed set of temporary teeth immediately after your implants are secured.

Single tooth dental implants are a reliable, long-term solution that look, feel, and function like natural teeth. Unlike natural teeth, implants are resistant to cavities and help preserve the jawbone, supporting both your oral health and your confidence. --- Same Day Teeth ---
Same Day Teeth Get New Teeth the Same Day as Your Implant Surgery Do you want a full arch or full mouth of dental implants but worry about going without teeth during the healing process.

This means no gaps in your smile, no uncomfortable dentures, and no waiting for your confidence to return. What Are the Benefits of Same Day Teeth. Immediate results : Walk out of surgery with a complete set of teeth. Improved confidence : No need to hide your smile during the healing process. Enhanced comfort : No removable dentures or adhesives. Natural appearance : Your temporary teeth look and feel like real teeth.

Jawbone preservation : Just like traditional implants, Same Day Teeth help maintain bone density. Our team will evaluate your oral health, bone density, and overall medical history to determine if you’re a candidate. Many patients qualify and are amazed at how seamless the process can be. Your Teeth on Surgery Day Are Not the Final Version One of the most common surprises is learning that the teeth placed on the day of surgery are temporary. You will leave with a full smile, but those teeth are designed to protect the implants while your mouth heals. This phase allows the dental team to focus on comfort, stability, and proper integration.

Once healing is complete, you receive your final set of teeth. These are custom-made for strength, fit, and long-term use. Healing Takes Time, Even With All-on-4 Dental Implants All-on-4 dental implants provide immediate function, but healing still takes several months.

Bone Loss Does Not Automatically Disqualify You Many people believe bone loss means dental implants are not an option. That is not always true.

All-on-4 dental implants are designed to work around areas of reduced bone by placing implants at specific angles. Before treatment, your provider will evaluate your bone structure, bite, and overall oral health. This planning process is one reason All-on-4 dental implants in Frisco work well for many patients who have been missing teeth for years.
